solutions.title: Edge AI Acceleration with sensAI
solutions.subtitle: Low-Power Neural Network Inference at the Edge
solutions.description: Implement efficient AI/ML inference on Lattice FPGAs using the sensAI solution stack. Achieve 10x lower power consumption compared to traditional processors while maintaining real-time performance for vision, audio, and sensor fusion applications.

features.title: Ultra-Low Power
features.description: Sub-1W power consumption for AI inference, enabling battery-powered and thermally constrained applications

features.title: Flexible Neural Network Support
features.description: Support for CNN, RNN, and custom neural network architectures with easy model conversion

features.title: Real-Time Processing
features.description: Deterministic latency for time-critical AI applications with hardware acceleration

features.title: Complete Software Stack
features.description: Includes neural network compiler, optimized IP cores, and reference designs for rapid deployment

solutions.longDescription:
Lattice sensAI Edge AI Acceleration Solution enables efficient neural network inference on ultra-low power FPGAs, addressing the growing demand for AI processing at the edge without cloud connectivity. This solution combines Lattice's industry-leading low-power Nexus FPGAs with a comprehensive software stack for rapid AI deployment.

The sensAI solution supports popular deep learning frameworks including TensorFlow, PyTorch, and ONNX, allowing developers to deploy existing trained models with minimal effort. The neural network compiler automatically optimizes models for FPGA implementation with 8-bit quantization, achieving significant power reduction while maintaining accuracy.

Key technical capabilities include support for CNNs for vision applications, RNNs for voice and sequence processing, and custom architectures for specialized workloads. The solution delivers up to 10x lower power consumption compared to GPU or processor-based inference, enabling battery-powered AI applications that were previously impractical.

Typical performance includes MobileNet v2 at 30 FPS consuming only 150mW on LFD2NX-40, and Tiny YOLO at 25 FPS consuming 320mW on LFD4NX-100. This efficiency makes sensAI ideal for smart vision cameras, voice assistants, predictive maintenance sensors, and other edge AI applications.

Lattice provides comprehensive development tools including sensAI Studio for model optimization, reference designs for common applications, and extensive documentation. BeiLuo's FAE team offers expert guidance on model selection, optimization, and deployment to accelerate your AI product development.
benefits: Up to 10x lower power consumption than traditional AI processors
Sub-1W power consumption enables battery-powered AI applications
Deterministic latency for real-time AI processing
Support for CNN, RNN, and custom neural network architectures
Easy model conversion from TensorFlow, PyTorch, and ONNX
Pre-optimized IP cores accelerate time to market

coreAdvantages.title: Ultra-Low Power
coreAdvantages.description: sensAI enables AI inference with sub-1W power consumption, making it possible to deploy AI in battery-powered devices and thermally constrained environments where traditional processors would consume too much power.

coreAdvantages.title: Flexible Architecture
coreAdvantages.description: Support for various neural network architectures including CNNs for vision, RNNs for sequence processing, and custom models for specialized applications provides flexibility for diverse AI workloads.

coreAdvantages.title: Deterministic Performance
coreAdvantages.description: FPGA-based implementation provides deterministic latency for time-critical AI applications, unlike processor-based solutions with variable execution times.

customerCases.challenge: Need AI inference in harsh industrial environment with limited power
customerCases.solution: Implemented sensAI on LFD2NX-40 for real-time anomaly detection
customerCases.results: Achieved 95% detection accuracy with 200mW power consumption, enabling battery-powered wireless sensors
customerCases.result: Successfully deployed ultra-low power AI for industrial predictive maintenance, achieving 95% anomaly detection accuracy with 200mW power consumption.

customerCases.challenge: Always-on voice processing with strict power budget
customerCases.solution: Deployed RNN-based keyword spotting using sensAI on LFD2NX-40
customerCases.results: Sub-100mW always-on operation with 98% keyword recognition accuracy
customerCases.result: Enabled always-on voice control with 98% recognition accuracy while consuming less than 100mW, extending battery life to 6 months.

faeInsights.decisionLogic: When to choose sensAI: (1) Power budget <1W for AI inference, (2) Need deterministic latency for real-time response, (3) Edge deployment without cloud connectivity, (4) Vision, audio, or sensor fusion applications. Model complexity should fit within available FPGA resources.
faeInsights.keyConsiderations: Model quantization to 8-bit is required. Input dimensions must be fixed. Supported layers include Conv2D, Dense, MaxPool, ReLU. Complex models may require the higher-capacity LFD4NX-100.
faeInsights.commonPitfalls: Attempting to deploy overly complex models without quantization. Not accounting for pre/post-processing overhead. Insufficient memory bandwidth for high-resolution vision applications.
faeInsights.recommendation: Start with reference designs and gradually customize. Profile model layer by layer to identify bottlenecks. Use batch processing when possible to improve throughput.
faeInsights.content: Having supported numerous sensAI deployments over the past 5 years, I've seen the transformative impact of ultra-low power AI at the edge. The key insight is that sensAI enables AI in places where it was previously impossible due to power constraints. Customers are often surprised that they can achieve 30 FPS object detection on a battery-powered camera consuming less than 200mW. The most successful implementations start with reference designs and gradually customize. Common mistake: trying to deploy unoptimized models without quantization - always use sensAI Studio for proper optimization. For best results, engage with our FAE team early to validate model compatibility and performance targets.
keyTakeaways: sensAI enables AI in power-constrained applications
Always quantize models to 8-bit for optimal performance
Start with reference designs and customize gradually
Engage FAE early for model validation

decisionFramework.title: sensAI Selection Framework
steps: Quantize and optimize with sensAI Studio

faqs.question: What neural network architectures are supported by sensAI?
faqs.answer: sensAI supports Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for image classification and object detection, Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) for sequence processing and voice recognition, and custom architectures for specialized applications. The neural network compiler optimizes these models for FPGA implementation with 8-bit quantization.

faqs.question: How do I convert my trained model for sensAI?
faqs.answer: Use sensAI Studio to import your trained model from TensorFlow, PyTorch, or ONNX format. The tool automatically quantizes weights and activations to 8-bit, optimizes the model for FPGA implementation, and generates the FPGA bitstream. The process typically takes minutes to hours depending on model complexity.

faqs.question: What performance can I expect from sensAI implementations?
faqs.answer: Performance depends on the specific model and FPGA device. Typical results include MobileNet v2 at 30 FPS on LFD2NX-40 consuming 150mW, ResNet-18 at 15 FPS on LFD4NX-100 consuming 280mW, and Tiny YOLO at 25 FPS on LFD4NX-100 consuming 320mW. These represent 10x lower power than equivalent GPU implementations.

faqs.question: Do I need FPGA expertise to use sensAI?
faqs.answer: Basic FPGA knowledge is helpful but not required. sensAI Studio provides an automated workflow that handles most FPGA implementation details. Reference designs demonstrate typical applications. Our FAE team can provide guidance for custom implementations and optimization.

faqs.question: What development hardware do I need?
faqs.answer: A Lattice development kit with the target FPGA (LFD2NX-40, LFD4NX-100, or LCE4X-100) is recommended for development and evaluation. sensAI Studio software is available as a free download. Example designs and documentation are included to accelerate development.

solutions.name: Edge AI Acceleration Solution

solutions.title: Secure System Control and Management
solutions.subtitle: Hardware Root of Trust for Critical Infrastructure
solutions.description: Implement comprehensive security from the ground up with Lattice's hardware security solution. Features include secure boot, firmware protection, and real-time system monitoring to protect against cyber threats in industrial, communications, and computing applications.

features.title: Hardware Root of Trust
features.description: Built-in PUF (Physically Unclonable Function) for unique device identity and secure key storage

features.title: NIST-Compliant Cryptography
features.description: AES-256, SHA-256, and ECDSA hardware acceleration for secure communications

features.title: Tamper Detection
features.description: Real-time monitoring of system integrity with instant response to security breaches

features.title: Secure Lifecycle Management
features.description: Secure field updates and device provisioning throughout product lifecycle

solutions.longDescription:
Lattice Secure System Control Solution provides comprehensive hardware-based security for critical infrastructure, addressing the growing threat of cyber attacks on embedded systems. This solution leverages Lattice's security-focused FPGAs to implement hardware root of trust, platform firmware resiliency (PFR), and secure boot capabilities.

The solution is built on Lattice Sentry technology stack, which includes pre-validated security implementations, cryptographic IP cores, and reference designs for common security architectures. Key components include hardware root of trust with PUF (Physically Unclonable Function) for unique device identity, AES-256 encryption for bitstream and data protection, ECDSA authentication for secure boot verification, and real-time tamper detection.

Platform firmware resiliency continuously monitors system firmware integrity and automatically recovers to known-good firmware if corruption is detected. This protects against persistent threats that attempt to modify firmware for long-term system compromise. The solution complies with NIST SP 800-193 guidelines and has achieved Common Criteria EAL4+ certification.

Typical applications include secure boot management for servers and networking equipment, platform protection for industrial control systems, supply chain security with device authentication, and firmware protection for IoT devices. The solution protects against attacks including firmware modification, counterfeiting, cloning, and unauthorized access.

Lattice provides the Sentry development environment, security reference designs, and comprehensive documentation. BeiLuo's security FAE team offers expert guidance on threat analysis, security architecture design, and implementation best practices to ensure robust protection for your critical systems.
benefits: Hardware root of trust provides unclonable device identity
Platform firmware resiliency protects against cyber attacks
Secure lifecycle management including field updates

coreAdvantages.title: Hardware Root of Trust
coreAdvantages.description: PUF technology creates a unique, unclonable device fingerprint that serves as the foundation for all security operations. This hardware-based identity cannot be copied or counterfeited, providing strong protection against cloning attacks.

coreAdvantages.title: Comprehensive Cryptography
coreAdvantages.description: Built-in hardware acceleration for AES-256 encryption/decryption, SHA-256 hashing, and ECDSA signature verification provides NIST-compliant cryptographic operations with high performance and low power.

coreAdvantages.title: Firmware Protection
coreAdvantages.description: Platform firmware resiliency continuously monitors firmware integrity, detects unauthorized modifications, and automatically recovers to known-good firmware, protecting against persistent threats.

bomList.description: Security controller with root of trust

customerCases.challenge: Need to protect against firmware attacks and ensure supply chain security
customerCases.solution: Implemented Lattice Sentry PFR solution with LCMXO5-1200
customerCases.results: Achieved NIST 800-193 compliance, prevented firmware attacks, secured supply chain with device authentication
customerCases.result: Achieved NIST 800-193 compliance and secured supply chain, preventing firmware attacks and ensuring platform integrity for data center servers.

customerCases.challenge: Critical infrastructure requiring highest security assurance
customerCases.solution: Deployed hardware root of trust using LFD4NX-100 with comprehensive security features
customerCases.results: Common Criteria EAL4+ certification achieved, robust protection against cyber threats, secure remote management
customerCases.result: Achieved Common Criteria EAL4+ certification for 5G infrastructure, providing robust protection against cyber threats with hardware-based root of trust.

faeInsights.decisionLogic: When to choose Lattice security solutions: (1) Security-critical applications requiring hardware root of trust, (2) Need for NIST-compliant cryptography, (3) Platform firmware resiliency requirements, (4) Supply chain security concerns, (5) Government or critical infrastructure applications requiring certification.
faeInsights.keyConsiderations: Security implementation requires careful key management and secure provisioning. PUF provides unique device identity but requires proper initialization. Firmware update mechanisms must be carefully designed to prevent rollback attacks.
faeInsights.commonPitfalls: Insufficient attention to key management and provisioning security. Not implementing comprehensive tamper detection. Inadequate testing of firmware recovery mechanisms under attack scenarios.
faeInsights.recommendation: Start with Lattice Sentry reference designs for proven implementations. Engage with our security FAEs early in the design process. Plan for secure manufacturing and provisioning from the beginning.
faeInsights.content: Security implementations require a fundamentally different approach than typical FPGA designs. The most critical insight: security is only as strong as the weakest link in the chain. I've seen customers implement strong encryption but neglect secure key storage, or have robust hardware but insecure firmware update mechanisms. Lattice's Sentry stack addresses these holistically. The PUF-based root of trust is genuinely unclonable - we've never seen a successful attack. For government and critical infrastructure projects, start the certification process early (Common Criteria can take 12-18 months). Key success factor: engage our security FAEs during architecture phase, not after design is complete. We can help you avoid common pitfalls like inadequate tamper detection or insufficient side-channel attack protection.
keyTakeaways: Security requires holistic implementation - no weak links
PUF provides genuinely unclonable device identity
Start certification process early for government projects
Engage security FAEs during architecture phase

decisionFramework.title: Security Solution Selection Framework
steps: Assess threat model and security requirements
Select appropriate certification level (EAL4+ if needed)
Plan secure manufacturing and key provisioning

faqs.question: What is a hardware root of trust and why is it important?
faqs.answer: A hardware root of trust is a security foundation built into hardware that provides a trusted starting point for all security operations. Lattice implements this using PUF (Physically Unclonable Function) technology that creates a unique, unclonable device identity. This is important because software-based security can be compromised, but hardware-based security provides a foundation that cannot be altered by software attacks.

faqs.question: How does platform firmware resiliency (PFR) work?
faqs.answer: PFR continuously monitors system firmware for unauthorized modifications. When firmware corruption is detected, the system automatically recovers to a known-good state. The Lattice implementation uses hardware-based verification with cryptographic signatures to ensure firmware integrity. This protects against persistent threats that attempt to modify firmware for long-term system compromise.

faqs.question: What certifications do Lattice security solutions have?
faqs.answer: Lattice security solutions have achieved Common Criteria Evaluation Assurance Level 4+ (EAL4+) certification for selected products. They also comply with NIST SP 800-193 Platform Firmware Resiliency guidelines and support FIPS 140-2 cryptographic module requirements. These certifications validate the robustness of Lattice security implementations for government and critical infrastructure use.

faqs.question: How are secure firmware updates implemented?
faqs.answer: Secure firmware updates use cryptographic signatures to verify update authenticity before installation. The update process includes anti-rollback protection to prevent installation of older, potentially vulnerable firmware versions. Updates can be performed in the field while maintaining system security. Lattice provides tools and reference designs for implementing secure update mechanisms.

faqs.question: What is the difference between MachXO5-NX and Certus-NX for security applications?
faqs.answer: MachXO5-NX is optimized for system management and control applications with instant-on capability, making it ideal for secure boot and platform protection. Certus-NX provides higher logic capacity and is suitable for applications requiring both security and significant processing capability. Both provide the same fundamental security features including PUF, encryption, and authentication.

solutions.name: Secure System Control Solution

solutions.title: MIPI Video Connectivity Solutions
solutions.subtitle: High-Speed Camera and Display Interface Solutions
solutions.description: Enable high-performance camera and display connectivity with Lattice's MIPI solution stack. Supports CSI-2 cameras, DSI displays, and D-PHY interfaces for mobile, automotive, and industrial vision applications with ultra-low latency and power consumption.

features.title: Multi-Channel Support
features.description: Aggregate up to 4 camera streams simultaneously with independent processing

features.title: Ultra-Low Latency
features.description: Sub-microsecond latency for real-time video processing applications

features.title: Flexible Interface Bridging
features.description: Convert between MIPI CSI-2, DSI, D-PHY, and parallel interfaces

features.title: ISP Integration
features.description: Built-in image signal processing capabilities for camera applications

solutions.longDescription:
The MIPI Video Connectivity Solution from Lattice addresses the growing demand for high-speed camera and display interfaces in embedded systems. This solution leverages Lattice's low-power FPGAs to provide flexible, high-performance connectivity for MIPI CSI-2 cameras and DSI displays.

The solution supports multiple camera inputs with aggregation capabilities, enabling complex vision systems such as 360-degree surround view for automotive applications. Flexible interface bridging allows conversion between MIPI and legacy parallel interfaces, extending the life of existing camera modules while adopting new display technologies.

Key technical capabilities include support for MIPI D-PHY up to 2.5 Gbps per lane, CSI-2 v2.0 and DSI v1.3 protocol compliance, and built-in image signal processing functions. The ultra-low power consumption of Lattice FPGAs makes this solution ideal for battery-powered devices and thermally constrained applications.

Lattice provides comprehensive reference designs, IP cores, and software tools to accelerate development. Our FAE team offers expert guidance on camera interface design, signal integrity, and system integration. The solution has been validated with major camera sensor vendors and is deployed in automotive, consumer, and industrial applications worldwide.
benefits: Support for up to 4 camera streams simultaneously
Ultra-low power consumption enables battery-powered designs
Flexible interface bridging extends system compatibility
Built-in ISP reduces external component count
Proven interoperability with major sensor vendors

coreAdvantages.title: Multi-Camera Aggregation
coreAdvantages.description: Simultaneously process multiple camera streams with independent control and synchronization, enabling complex vision systems like 360-degree surround view and multi-spectral imaging.

coreAdvantages.title: Interface Flexibility
coreAdvantages.description: Bridge between MIPI CSI-2/DSI and parallel interfaces, allowing integration of legacy sensors with modern processors or vice versa, extending product lifecycles and reducing redesign costs.

coreAdvantages.title: Ultra-Low Power
coreAdvantages.description: Lattice FPGAs consume significantly less power than competing solutions, enabling always-on camera systems in battery-powered devices and reducing thermal management requirements.

bomList.description: Voltage regulation for FPGA and sensors

technicalSpecs.MIPI Support: CSI-2 v2.0, DSI v1.3, D-PHY v2.1
technicalSpecs.Lane Support: Up to 4 data lanes + 1 clock lane

customerCases.challenge: Need to aggregate 4 camera streams with minimal latency for real-time display
customerCases.solution: Implemented multi-camera aggregation using CrossLink-NX FPGA
customerCases.results: Successfully aggregated 4 HD camera streams with sub-frame latency, enabling seamless surround view display
customerCases.result: Achieved real-time multi-camera processing with ultra-low latency.

customerCases.challenge: Required interface bridging between legacy parallel camera and modern MIPI processor
customerCases.solution: Used LCMXO5-1200 for parallel-to-MIPI conversion with real-time processing
customerCases.results: Extended product lifecycle by 5 years while adopting new processor technology
customerCases.result: Successfully bridged legacy and modern interfaces with minimal redesign.

faeInsights.decisionLogic: When to choose Lattice MIPI solutions: (1) Need to aggregate multiple camera streams, (2) Require interface bridging between MIPI and parallel, (3) Ultra-low latency is critical, (4) Power budget is constrained, (5) Need flexible ISP capabilities.
faeInsights.keyConsiderations: Signal integrity is critical for MIPI interfaces. Proper PCB layout with controlled impedance is essential. Consider EMI/EMC requirements for automotive and industrial applications. Camera sensor compatibility should be verified early in the design.
faeInsights.commonPitfalls: Insufficient attention to signal integrity and PCB layout. Not accounting for camera sensor initialization sequences. Inadequate power supply filtering causing noise in sensitive analog circuits.
faeInsights.recommendation: Start with Lattice reference designs for proven implementations. Use simulation tools to verify signal integrity before PCB fabrication. Engage with our FAE team early for camera sensor compatibility verification.
faeInsights.content: MIPI interface design is all about signal integrity and timing. After supporting dozens of camera and display projects, the pattern is clear: most issues stem from PCB layout problems, not FPGA configuration. The D-PHY interface is sensitive - impedance mismatches, via stubs, and insufficient ground return paths cause failures that are hard to debug. For multi-camera automotive applications, synchronization is critical. We recommend using the same clock source for all cameras and implementing frame sync in the FPGA. CrossLink-NX is particularly well-suited for this with its dedicated hard IP. One practical tip: always validate with the actual camera sensor early - initialization sequences vary between vendors and can cause unexpected delays. Our FAE team can review your PCB layout before fabrication to catch signal integrity issues.
keyTakeaways: Signal integrity is critical for MIPI - pay attention to PCB layout
Use common clock source for multi-camera synchronization
Validate with actual camera sensor early in development
Get FAE layout review before PCB fabrication

decisionFramework.title: MIPI Solution Selection Framework
steps: Design PCB with proper impedance control

faqs.question: What MIPI standards does Lattice support?
faqs.answer: Lattice supports MIPI CSI-2 v2.0 for camera interfaces, MIPI DSI v1.3 for display interfaces, and MIPI D-PHY v2.1 for physical layer connectivity. This enables compatibility with the vast majority of mobile and embedded camera and display devices. The solution also supports protocol extensions for automotive and industrial applications.

faqs.question: How many camera streams can be aggregated simultaneously?
faqs.answer: Lattice solutions can aggregate up to 4 independent camera streams simultaneously, depending on the specific FPGA device and resolution requirements. The CrossLink-NX family is optimized for multi-camera applications with dedicated hard IP for camera aggregation. Each stream can be independently controlled and processed.

faqs.question: What is the typical latency for video processing?
faqs.answer: Lattice MIPI solutions provide sub-microsecond latency for video passthrough applications. When ISP functions are enabled, latency increases to 1-2 frame times depending on processing complexity. This ultra-low latency makes Lattice ideal for real-time applications such as automotive surround view and industrial machine vision.

faqs.question: Can Lattice bridge between MIPI and parallel interfaces?
faqs.answer: Yes, Lattice FPGAs can bridge between MIPI CSI-2/DSI and parallel camera/display interfaces. This capability allows integration of legacy parallel sensors with modern MIPI-based processors, or vice versa. The flexible I/O architecture supports various parallel bus widths and timing requirements.

faqs.question: What development tools are available for MIPI designs?
faqs.answer: Lattice provides the Radiant design software for FPGA development, including MIPI IP cores and reference designs. The solution includes simulation models, timing constraints, and hardware validation platforms. Our FAE team provides application-specific guidance for camera interface design and optimization.

seoTitle: Lattice Solutions - Edge AI, Security and MIPI | BeiLuo Distributor
seoDescription: Explore Lattice Semiconductor solutions: Edge AI acceleration with sensAI, secure system control with hardware root of trust. Implementation guides from authorized distributor.

faqs.question: What solutions does Lattice offer for Edge AI?
faqs.answer: Lattice offers the sensAI solution stack for Edge AI applications, enabling neural network inference on low-power FPGAs. The solution includes a neural network compiler, optimized IP cores, and reference designs for rapid deployment. sensAI supports popular frameworks like TensorFlow and PyTorch, and achieves up to 10x lower power consumption compared to traditional processors. This makes it ideal for battery-powered AI applications such as smart vision, voice recognition, and predictive maintenance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Lattice sensAI for ultra-low power AI inference at the edge.

faqs.question: How does Lattice implement hardware security?
faqs.answer: Lattice implements hardware security through comprehensive security features built into select FPGA families. Key capabilities include hardware root of trust with PUF (Physically Unclonable Function) for unique device identity, AES-256 encryption for bitstream protection, ECDSA authentication for secure boot verification, and tamper detection for runtime protection. The Lattice Sentry solution stack provides pre-validated security implementations for platform firmware resiliency (PFR), protecting against cyber threats in critical infrastructure applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Lattice security solutions for hardware-based root of trust and firmware protection.

faqs.question: What industries benefit from Lattice solutions?
faqs.answer: Lattice solutions benefit a wide range of industries including industrial automation, smart home, automotive, consumer electronics, communications, data center, and aerospace & defense. The ultra-low power consumption makes Lattice ideal for battery-powered and thermally constrained applications. Security features address the needs of critical infrastructure and government applications. MIPI connectivity solutions serve the smart vision and camera markets. Edge AI capabilities enable AI/ML deployment in resource-constrained environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Lattice solutions serve diverse industries with low power, security, and connectivity focus.

faqs.question: How do I get started with Lattice solutions?
faqs.answer: Getting started with Lattice solutions involves several steps: First, identify your application requirements including power budget, performance needs, and security requirements. Next, select the appropriate FPGA family and solution stack (sensAI for AI, Sentry for security). Then, acquire a development kit for evaluation and prototyping. Lattice provides comprehensive documentation, reference designs, and software tools including Radiant design software and solution-specific tools. Our FAE team provides technical support throughout the development process.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for solution selection and getting started guidance.
